 {"id":"bd-1hnyy","title":"Cost Attribution Custom Tags","description":"## Overview

User-defined tags on agents/sessions that flow to cost records, enabling custom slicing of cost data (by feature, sprint, team, experiment).

## Background & Justification

Cost tracking already has dimensions:
- Organization
- Project
- Agent
- Task
- Session

But these are fixed. Teams need custom dimensions:
- Feature (which feature is this work for?)
- Sprint (which sprint to bill?)
- Team (which team owns this cost?)
- Experiment (A/B testing cost comparison)

Custom tags enable project-specific cost analysis without code changes.

## Key Components

### 1. Tag Data Model

```typescript
interface CostTag {
  key: string;    // e.g., "feature", "sprint", "team"
  value: string;  // e.g., "auth-v2", "sprint-42", "platform"
}

// Tags can be set at multiple levels
interface TagScope {
  scope: 'agent' | 'session' | 'task' | 'manual';
  agentId?: string;
  sessionId?: string;
  taskId?: string;
}
```

### 2. Tag Propagation

Tags flow to cost records:
- Agent-level tags → all costs for that agent
- Session-level tags → costs during that session
- Task-level tags → costs for specific task
- Manual tags → applied retroactively

Inheritance: Agent > Session > Task (more specific wins)

### 3. Tag Management

**Setting Tags:**
- UI: Tag editor on agent/session detail
- API: Set tags on creation or update
- CLI: `--tags feature:auth,sprint:42`

**Tag Validation:**
- Key: alphanumeric + underscore, max 50 chars
- Value: any string, max 100 chars
- Max 10 tags per scope

### 4. Cost Analytics Integration

Extend cost queries:
```sql
-- Costs by feature
SELECT tag_value, SUM(cost_millicents) 
FROM cost_records 
JOIN cost_tags ON ...
WHERE tag_key = 'feature'
GROUP BY tag_value;

-- Compare experiments
SELECT 
  tag_value AS experiment,
  SUM(cost_millicents) AS total_cost,
  COUNT(DISTINCT agent_id) AS agents
FROM cost_records
JOIN cost_tags ON ...
WHERE tag_key = 'experiment'
GROUP BY tag_value;
```

### 5. UI Components

**Tag Editor:**
- Key-value input fields
- Autocomplete for existing keys
- Add/remove tags

**Cost Filter by Tag:**
- Tag key dropdown
- Tag value multi-select
- "Group by tag" option

**Tag-based Reports:**
- Cost by tag key chart
- Tag value breakdown table
- Tag trend over time

### 6. API Extensions

- `POST /api/agents/:id/tags` - Set agent tags
- `GET /api/agents/:id/tags` - Get agent tags
- `POST /api/sessions/:id/tags` - Set session tags
- `GET /api/cost-analytics/by-tag` - Query costs by tag
- `GET /api/tags/keys` - List used tag keys
- `GET /api/tags/values/:key` - List values for key

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE cost_tags (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  cost_record_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  tag_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  tag_value TEXT NOT NULL,
  source_scope TEXT NOT NULL, -- agent, session, task, manual
  source_id TEXT,
  created_at TEXT
);

CREATE INDEX idx_cost_tags_key ON cost_tags(tag_key);
CREATE INDEX idx_cost_tags_key_value ON cost_tags(tag_key, tag_value);

CREATE TABLE tag_definitions (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  description TEXT,
  allowed_values TEXT, -- JSON array, null = any
  created_at TEXT
);
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. Design tag data model and schema
2. Add tag tables to database
3. Extend agent/session APIs with tag endpoints
4. Implement tag propagation to cost records
5. Extend cost analytics queries for tag filtering
6. Build tag editor UI component
7. Add tag filters to cost analytics UI
8. Create tag-based report views
9. Implement tag autocomplete
10. Write unit tests for tag propagation
11. Write integration tests for cost queries
12. Write E2E test for tag flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tags can be set on agents, sessions, tasks
- [ ] Tags propagate to cost records automatically
- [ ] Cost analytics can filter/group by tags
- [ ] Tag autocomplete shows existing keys
- [ ] Reports break down costs by tag values
- [ ] Tag inheritance works correctly

## Dependencies

-{"id":"bd-1kij1","title":"Feature Flag System","description":"## Overview

Infrastructure for gradual feature rollout with percentage-based targeting, user cohorts, and kill switches. Enables safer deployments and A/B testing.

## Background & Justification

Currently all features are either on or off for everyone. Problems:
- Risky to deploy new features to all users
- No A/B testing capability
- No way to quickly disable problematic features
- No gradual rollout for stability

Feature flags enable:
- Deploy code with feature off, enable gradually
- Target specific users for beta testing
- Kill switch for rapid disable
- Metrics per flag for comparison

## Key Components

### 1. Flag Data Model

```typescript
interface FeatureFlag {
  key: string; // e.g., "new-dashboard-v2"
  name: string;
  description: string;
  
  // State
  enabled: boolean;
  
  // Targeting
  targeting: {
    type: 'all' | 'percentage' | 'users' | 'cohorts' | 'rules';
    percentage?: number; // 0-100
    userIds?: string[];
    cohortIds?: string[];
    rules?: TargetingRule[];
  };
  
  // Variants (for A/B)
  variants?: Variant[];
  
  // Metadata
  createdAt: Date;
  modifiedAt: Date;
  createdBy: string;
}

interface TargetingRule {
  field: string; // e.g., "user.email", "agent.type"
  operator: 'eq' | 'neq' | 'contains' | 'startsWith' | 'endsWith' | 'matches';
  value: string | string[];
}

interface Variant {
  key: string;
  weight: number; // 0-100
  value: any;
}
```

### 2. Flag Evaluation

```typescript
function evaluateFlag(
  flag: FeatureFlag,
  context: EvaluationContext
): FlagResult {
  if (!flag.enabled) return { enabled: false };
  
  switch (flag.targeting.type) {
    case 'all':
      return { enabled: true };
    case 'percentage':
      return { enabled: hashInPercentage(context.userId, flag.key, flag.targeting.percentage) };
    case 'users':
      return { enabled: flag.targeting.userIds.includes(context.userId) };
    case 'cohorts':
      return { enabled: userInAnyCohort(context.userId, flag.targeting.cohortIds) };
    case 'rules':
      return { enabled: evaluateRules(flag.targeting.rules, context) };
  }
}
```

### 3. Cohort System

```typescript
interface Cohort {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  description: string;
  type: 'static' | 'dynamic';
  
  // For static
  userIds?: string[];
  
  // For dynamic
  rules?: TargetingRule[];
}
```

Examples:
- "Beta Testers" - static list of user IDs
- "Power Users" - dynamic: agents > 10
- "Enterprise" - dynamic: org.plan == "enterprise"

### 4. UI Components

**Flag List:**
- All flags with status
- Quick toggle
- Filter by state

**Flag Editor:**
- Enable/disable toggle
- Targeting configuration
- Variant weights
- Preview evaluation

**Cohort Manager:**
- Create/edit cohorts
- Member preview
- Usage in flags

**Analytics:**
- Flag evaluation counts
- Variant distribution
- Error rates per variant

### 5. SDK Integration

```typescript
// In code
if (await flags.isEnabled('new-dashboard-v2', { userId: user.id })) {
  // New code path
} else {
  // Old code path
}

// With variants
const variant = await flags.getVariant('button-color', { userId: user.id });
buttonColor = variant.value;
```

### 6. API Endpoints

- `GET /api/flags` - List all flags
- `GET /api/flags/:key` - Flag detail
- `POST /api/flags` - Create flag
- `PUT /api/flags/:key` - Update flag
- `DELETE /api/flags/:key` - Delete flag
- `POST /api/flags/:key/evaluate` - Evaluate flag for context
- `GET /api/flags/:key/metrics` - Flag metrics
- CRUD for cohorts

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE feature_flags (
  key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  enabled INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  targeting TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON
  variants TEXT, -- JSON
  created_by TEXT,
  created_at TEXT,
  modified_at TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE cohorts (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,
  config TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON
  created_at TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE flag_evaluations (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  flag_key TEXT NOT NULL,
  user_id TEXT,
  result INTEGER,
  variant_key TEXT,
  evaluated_at TEXT
);
```

## Implementation Tasks

1. Design flag data model
2. Implement flag evaluation engine
3. Implement consistent hashing for percentage rollout
4. Create cohort system
5. Add API routes for flag management
6. Build flag list UI
7. Build flag editor UI
8. Build cohort manager UI
9. Implement SDK helpers for code integration
10. Add evaluation metrics collection
11. Build analytics dashboard
12. Write unit tests for evaluation
13. Write E2E test for flag flow

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Flags can be toggled on/off
- [ ] Percentage rollout is consistent (same user always gets same result)
- [ ] User targeting works correctly
- [ ] Cohort targeting works
- [ ] Rules evaluate correctly
- [ ] Variants distribute by weight
- [ ] Metrics track evaluations
- [ ] UI allows full flag management

## Dependencies

+{"id":"bd-1kij1","title":"Feature Flag System","description":"## Overview

Infrastructure for gradual feature rollout with percentage-based targeting, user cohorts, and kill switches. Enables safer deployments, A/B testing, and rapid feature disable in emergencies.

## Background & Justification

Currently all features are either on or off for everyone. Problems:
- Risky to deploy new features to all users at once
- No A/B testing capability for comparing implementations
- No way to quickly disable problematic features
- No gradual rollout for stability monitoring

Feature flags enable:
- Deploy code with feature off, enable gradually (1% → 10% → 50% → 100%)
- Target specific users for beta testing
- Kill switch for rapid disable without redeployment
- Metrics per flag for comparison and decision making

## Key Components

### 1. Flag Data Model

```typescript
interface FeatureFlag {
  key: string; // e.g., "new-dashboard-v2"
  name: string;
  description: string;
  
  // State
  enabled: boolean;
  
  // Targeting
  targeting: {
    type: 'all' | 'percentage' | 'users' | 'cohorts' | 'rules';
    percentage?: number; // 0-100
    userIds?: string[];
    cohortIds?: string[];
    rules?: TargetingRule[];
  };
  
  // Variants (for A/B testing)
  variants?: Variant[];
  defaultVariant?: string;
  
  // Metadata
  createdAt: Date;
  modifiedAt: Date;
  createdBy: string;
  
  // Lifecycle
  expiresAt?: Date;
  staleAfter?: Date; // Warning if flag still exists after this date
}

interface TargetingRule {
  field: string; // e.g., "user.email", "agent.type", "org.plan"
  operator: 'eq' | 'neq' | 'contains' | 'startsWith' | 'endsWith' | 'matches' | 'in' | 'notIn';
  value: string | string[];
}

interface Variant {
  key: string;
  weight: number; // 0-100, all weights must sum to 100
  value: any;
  description?: string;
}
```

### 2. Flag Evaluation Engine

```typescript
interface EvaluationContext {
  userId?: string;
  orgId?: string;
  agentId?: string;
  sessionId?: string;
  userEmail?: string;
  userPlan?: string;
  agentType?: string;
  customAttributes?: Record<string, any>;
}

interface FlagResult {
  enabled: boolean;
  variant?: string;
  value?: any;
  reason: 'targeting' | 'percentage' | 'default' | 'disabled' | 'expired';
}

function evaluateFlag(
  flag: FeatureFlag,
  context: EvaluationContext
): FlagResult {
  // Check expiry
  if (flag.expiresAt && new Date() > flag.expiresAt) {
    return { enabled: false, reason: 'expired' };
  }
  
  if (!flag.enabled) {
    return { enabled: false, reason: 'disabled' };
  }
  
  switch (flag.targeting.type) {
    case 'all':
      return evaluateVariants(flag, context);
    case 'percentage':
      const inPercentage = hashInPercentage(
        context.userId || context.sessionId,
        flag.key,
        flag.targeting.percentage
      );
      return inPercentage 
        ? evaluateVariants(flag, context)
        : { enabled: false, reason: 'percentage' };
    case 'users':
      const inUsers = flag.targeting.userIds?.includes(context.userId);
      return inUsers
        ? evaluateVariants(flag, context)
        : { enabled: false, reason: 'targeting' };
    case 'cohorts':
      const inCohort = userInAnyCohort(context.userId, flag.targeting.cohortIds);
      return inCohort
        ? evaluateVariants(flag, context)
        : { enabled: false, reason: 'targeting' };
    case 'rules':
      const matchesRules = evaluateRules(flag.targeting.rules, context);
      return matchesRules
        ? evaluateVariants(flag, context)
        : { enabled: false, reason: 'targeting' };
  }
}

// Consistent hashing for percentage rollout
function hashInPercentage(userId: string, flagKey: string, percentage: number): boolean {
  const hash = murmur3(`${userId}:${flagKey}`);
  const bucket = hash % 100;
  return bucket < percentage;
}
```

 {"id":"bd-2befy","title":"Reservation Negotiation Protocol","description":"## Overview

When file reservation conflicts occur, instead of just blocking, the system proposes resolution strategies and can automatically negotiate based on configured policies. This transforms multi-agent conflicts from blockers into collaborative workflows.

## Background & Justification

Currently when Agent A requests a file that Agent B holds:
- The request is simply rejected with a conflict error
- Agent A has no recourse except waiting indefinitely
- No visibility into whether B is actually using the file
- No automatic resolution

The reservation system already detects conflicts (`reservation-conflicts.ts`). MCP Agent Mail already enables agent-to-agent communication. The gap is the negotiation layer connecting them.

## Key Components

### 1. Negotiation Protocol

When conflict detected:

1. **Assess Situation:**
   - Is holder actively using the file? (recent activity timestamp)
   - How old is the reservation? (stale detection threshold)
   - What are task priorities? (priority-based preemption)
   - Is holder responsive? (Agent Mail delivery confirmation)

2. **Propose Resolution:**
   - `WAIT` - Wait for holder to release (with estimated time based on history)
   - `REQUEST_HANDOFF` - Ask holder to release via Agent Mail
   - `PRIORITY_PREEMPT` - Higher priority claims the file (with notification)
   - `FORCE_ACQUIRE` - Take with notification (admin-only, audit logged)
   - `SPLIT_SCOPE` - Propose non-overlapping file scopes

3. **Execute Resolution:**
   - Send Agent Mail negotiation request to holder
   - Track negotiation state machine
   - Handle response (approve/deny/timeout/counter-proposal)
   - Apply resolution outcome
   - Record audit trail

### 2. Automatic Policies

Configurable policies for common scenarios:

```typescript
interface NegotiationPolicy {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  priority: number; // Higher = evaluated first
  condition: {
    holderInactiveForSeconds?: number;
    reservationAgeSeconds?: number;
    requesterPriority?: 'higher' | 'same' | 'any';
    scopeOverlap?: 'full' | 'partial' | 'any';
    holderAgentType?: string[]; // e.g., ['claude', 'codex']
  };
  action: 'auto_release' | 'request_handoff' | 'priority_preempt' | 'wait';
  timeoutSeconds?: number; // Before escalation
  escalationAction?: 'notify_admin' | 'force_release' | 'none';
}

// Default policies (can be customized)
const defaultPolicies: NegotiationPolicy[] = [
  {
    id: 'stale_release',
    name: 'Auto-release stale reservations',
    priority: 100,
    condition: { holderInactiveForSeconds: 1800 }, // 30 min inactive
    action: 'auto_release'
  },
  {
    id: 'priority_preemption',
    name: 'Priority-based preemption',
    priority: 90,
    condition: { requesterPriority: 'higher', holderInactiveForSeconds: 300 },
    action: 'priority_preempt'
  },
  {
    id: 'polite_request',
    name: 'Request handoff for partial overlap',
    priority: 50,
    condition: { scopeOverlap: 'partial' },
    action: 'request_handoff',
    timeoutSeconds: 120,
    escalationAction: 'notify_admin'
  }
];
```

### 3. Agent Mail Integration

Negotiation messages via Agent Mail:

```markdown
Subject: [Reservation Request] src/services/*.ts

Hi GreenAgent,

BlueLake needs access to files you currently hold:
- Requested: src/services/foo.ts
- You hold: src/services/*.ts

**Reason:** Implementing new authentication feature
**Priority:** High (P1)
**Requested scope:** src/services/auth.ts only

Options:
1. [Release] Release the files
2. [Defer] Propose alternative timing
3. [Split] Suggest scope split
4. [Deny] Decline the request

Please respond within 2 minutes or the system will auto-resolve per policy.

---
Thread: negotiation-{id}
Ack-Required: true
```

### 4. Negotiation State Machine

```
INITIATED → PENDING → APPROVED | DENIED | TIMEOUT | COUNTER_PROPOSED
                               ↓
                           RESOLVED (with outcome)
                               or
                           ESCALATED (to admin/policy)
```

States:
- `INITIATED` - Negotiation created, assessing
- `PENDING` - Request sent, awaiting response
- `APPROVED` - Holder agreed to release
- `DENIED` - Holder declined
- `TIMEOUT` - No response within window
- `COUNTER_PROPOSED` - Holder proposed alternative
- `RESOLVED` - Final outcome applied
- `ESCALATED` - Sent to admin or policy engine
- `CANCELLED` - Requester cancelled

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE reservation_negotiations (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  reservation_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  requester_agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  holder_agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  requested_paths TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON array
  reason TEXT,
  priority TEXT,
  state TEXT NOT NULL,
  proposed_action TEXT,
  outcome TEXT,
  outcome_reason TEXT,
  mail_thread_id TEXT,
  timeout_at TEXT,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  responded_at TEXT,
  resolved_at TEXT,
  FOREIGN KEY (reservation_id) REFERENCES reservations(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (requester_agent_id) REFERENCES agents(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (holder_agent_id) REFERENCES agents(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_negotiations_state ON reservation_negotiations(state);
CREATE INDEX idx_negotiations_timeout ON reservation_negotiations(timeout_at) WHERE state = 'PENDING';

CREATE TABLE negotiation_policies (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 50,
  condition TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON
  action TEXT NOT NULL,
  timeout_seconds INTEGER,
  escalation_action TEXT,
  enabled INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
  created_at TEXT,
  updated_at TEXT
);
```

+{"id":"bd-62hcq","title":"Type-Safe Gateway Client SDK (Generated)","description":"## Overview

Provide a **generated, type-safe TypeScript SDK** for Flywheel Gateway so external tooling (and potentially the dashboard) can call the API with:

- correct request/response types
- stable error handling
- first-class support for streaming endpoints

The repo already contains a Command Registry + codegen scaffolding (`packages/shared/src/commands/codegen/client.ts`), but the generated types are currently placeholders (`unknown`) and there is no clearly shipped SDK package.

## Goals

- A published (workspace-local) package, e.g. `packages/gateway-sdk/`, that exposes:
  - typed methods for REST endpoints
  - typed helpers for streaming (SSE) endpoints
  - consistent auth handling (Bearer token / API key as applicable)
  - stable error objects (status, code, message, correlation/trace IDs)
- Generation is deterministic and runs in CI.

## Non-Goals

- Supporting every possible client runtime (focus: Bun + Node + browser fetch).
- Perfect parity with future tRPC surfaces (start with REST/OpenAPI or Command Registry, pick one).

## Proposed Approach

1. Decide the source of truth:
   - Option A: Command Registry (preferred if it is current + authoritative)
   - Option B: OpenAPI 3.1 schema
2. Implement real types:
   - infer input/output types from Zod schemas (if using registry)
   - or generate from OpenAPI (`openapi-typescript`) and wrap
3. Codegen outputs:
   - stable client surface (no breaking renames on minor route refactors)
   - streaming helpers with backpressure/cancellation
4. Add contract tests:
   - SDK calls at least a few endpoints against a real test app

## Test Plan

- Unit: codegen output snapshots + typing checks.
- Integration: run generated client against test gateway instance.
- CI: ensure generated code is up to date (fail if diff).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SDK types are not `unknown` for core endpoints.
- [ ] Streaming endpoints are usable ergonomically.
- [ ] Errors preserve status + message + correlation/trace IDs.
- [ ] Generation is deterministic and enforced in CI.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:20:28.530282715Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:20:28.558567414Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["client","dx"]}

 {"id":"bd-66wud","title":"RBAC and API Key Scopes","description":"## Overview

Add role-based access control with scoped API keys so different users and automations can access only the features they need.

## Background and Rationale

As teams grow, a single admin role is insufficient. Operators need read-only dashboards, developers need limited agent access, and automation keys should be narrowly scoped to reduce blast radius.

## Scope

- Roles and permission matrix
- Scoped API keys (read/write, per-resource)
- UI for role assignment and key management
- Permission enforcement across API and WebSocket

## Roles (Initial)

- **Owner**: full access
- **Admin**: manage agents, policies, dashboards
- **Developer**: manage own agents + sessions
- **Viewer**: read-only
- **Automation**: scoped key-only access

## Permission Model

```typescript
type Permission =
  | 'agents:read' | 'agents:write'
  | 'sessions:read' | 'sessions:write'
  | 'policies:read' | 'policies:write'
  | 'analytics:read'
  | 'admin:manage';

interface ApiKeyScope {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  permissions: Permission[];
  resourceFilters?: { agentIds?: string[]; workspaceIds?: string[] };
}
```

## API

- `GET /api/roles`
- `POST /api/roles`
- `PUT /api/roles/:id`
- `POST /api/users/:id/role`
- `POST /api/api-keys`
- `DELETE /api/api-keys/:id`

## UI

- Roles page with permission matrix
- API key creation + scope picker
- Audit log entries for role changes and key creation

## Implementation Tasks

1. Add role + permission schema
2. Implement auth middleware with permission checks
3. Add scoped API key verification
4. Update routes to enforce permissions
5. Build UI for role assignment and key management
6. Add audit logging for changes

## Testing

- Unit: permission checks (allow/deny)
- Integration: scoped keys limit access
- E2E: viewer cannot mutate, admin can

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Permissions enforced across REST and WS
- [ ] Scoped keys cannot access out-of-scope resources
- [ ] Role changes are logged and auditable
- [ ] UI clearly shows effective permissions","notes":"Revision pass 4 (supersedes prior notes):

Provide a first-class, queryable timeline of agent activity (state transitions, tool calls/results, outputs, errors, file ops) with a UI replay mode. This turns today’s scattered logs into a single narrative that can be audited, debugged, and shared.

## Why This Is Accretive
- Debugging and incident response become dramatically faster.
- Users can replay exactly what an agent did and why a failure occurred.
- Provides the foundation for analytics, SLA verification, and governance.

## Scope and Design
- Persist normalized agent events with stable schemas and redacted payloads.
- Provide cursor-based API access for efficient playback.
- UI timeline with filtering, grouping, and export.
- Retention and sampling controls for storage safety.

## Proposed API
- `GET /api/agents/:id/timeline?cursor=&limit=&types=`
- `GET /api/agents/:id/events/:eventId`
- `GET /api/agents/:id/timeline/export?format=json|csv`
- WebSocket channel `agent:{id}:timeline` (optional live feed)

## Data Model (SQLite)
```
agent_events (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL,          -- state, tool_call, tool_result, output, error, file
  severity TEXT,               -- info|warn|error
  occurred_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  payload TEXT NOT NULL,       -- JSON (redacted)
  correlation_id TEXT,
  session_id TEXT,
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL
)
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_events_agent_time ON agent_events(agent_id, occurred_at DESC);
```

## Implementation Plan
1. Add `agent_events` schema and migration.
2. Create `agent-event.service.ts` to normalize + store events.
3. Extend BaseDriver emit pipeline to forward events to service.
4. Add redaction layer for payloads (reuse audit redaction).
5. Implement retention policy (size and age based).
6. Build API routes with cursor pagination.
7. Add UI timeline page + filters + event detail drawer.
8. Add export endpoint and UI action.
9. Optional: WebSocket live feed for real-time replay.

## Testing
- Unit: event normalization, redaction, cursor pagination.
- Integration: event ingestion from driver state transitions.
- E2E: timeline page renders events and filters properly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline shows all key agent events in order.
- Cursor pagination is stable and deterministic.
- Payloads are redacted (no secrets in UI/export).
- Retention policy keeps DB bounded.

## Dependencies
- Existing driver event emission and audit redaction utilities.","notes":"Revision pass 4 (supersedes prior notes):

- Form for all template fields
- Live preview of resulting agent config
- Validation feedback
- "Save" / "Save as New" options

**Template Preview Modal:**
- Full configuration breakdown
- Comparison with defaults
- "Use This Template" CTA

### 5. API Endpoints

- `GET /api/templates` - List templates (filter by category, search)
- `GET /api/templates/:id` - Template detail
- `POST /api/templates` - Create custom template
- `PUT /api/templates/:id` - Update template
- `DELETE /api/templates/:id` - Delete template (soft delete)
- `POST /api/templates/:id/use` - Apply template to create agent
- `POST /api/templates/:id/clone` - Clone template for editing
- `POST /api/templates/import` - Import from JSON
- `GET /api/templates/:id/export` - Export to JSON
- `POST /api/templates/:id/rate` - Submit rating

## Database Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE agent_templates (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  description TEXT,
  category TEXT NOT NULL,
  icon TEXT,
  config TEXT NOT NULL, -- JSON
  environment_hints TEXT, -- JSON
  is_built_in INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_by TEXT,
  usage_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  total_rating INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  rating_count INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  version TEXT DEFAULT '1.0.0',
  deleted_at TEXT, -- soft delete
  created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
  updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE INDEX idx_templates_category ON agent_templates(category);
CREATE INDEX idx_templates_popularity ON agent_templates(usage_count DESC);
```

```typescript
describe('TemplateService', () => {
  describe('create', () => {
    it('should create template with valid config', async () => {
      const template = await service.create({
        name: 'My Template',
        category: 'custom',
        config: { agentType: 'claude', ... }
      });
      expect(template.id).toBeDefined();
      expect(template.version).toBe('1.0.0');
    });
    
    it('should reject template with invalid config', async () => {
      await expect(service.create({
        name: 'Bad Template',
        config: { agentType: 'invalid' }
      })).rejects.toThrow('Invalid agent type');
    });
    
    it('should auto-generate ID if not provided', async () => {
      const template = await service.create({ name: 'Test', ... });
      expect(template.id).toMatch(/^tpl_[a-z0-9]+$/);
    });
  });
  
  describe('apply', () => {
    it('should create agent with template config', async () => {
      const agent = await service.apply('tpl_refactoring', {
        name: 'My Refactor Agent'
      });
      expect(agent.modelConfig.model).toBe('claude-opus');
      expect(agent.contextConfig.maxContextTokens).toBe(200000);
    });
    
    it('should increment usage count on apply', async () => {
      const before = await service.get('tpl_refactoring');
      await service.apply('tpl_refactoring', { name: 'Test' });
      const after = await service.get('tpl_refactoring');
      expect(after.usageCount).toBe(before.usageCount + 1);
    });
  });
  
  describe('import/export', () => {
    it('should export template to JSON', async () => {
      const json = await service.export('tpl_testing');
      expect(json).toContain('"name"');
      expect(json).toContain('"config"');
    });
    
    it('should import valid JSON template', async () => {
      const template = await service.import(validJson);
      expect(template.id).toBeDefined();
    });
    
    it('should reject invalid JSON structure', async () => {
      await expect(service.import(invalidJson))
        .rejects.toThrow('Invalid template format');
    });
  });
});
```

```typescript
describe('Template API', () => {

    const body = await res.json();
    expect(body.templates.length).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(5);

      method: 'POST',
      body: JSON.stringify({ name: 'Test Agent' })
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(201);
    const agent = await res.json();
    expect(agent.name).toBe('Test Agent');
  });

      method: 'DELETE'
    });
    expect(res.status).toBe(403);
  });
});
```

### E2E Tests (`tests/e2e/agent-templates.spec.ts`)

```typescript
test.describe('Agent Templates', () => {
  test('should browse and filter templates', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/agents/new');
    await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="template-picker"]')).toBeVisible();
    
    // Filter by category
    await page.click('[data-testid="category-testing"]');
    await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="template-card"]')).toHaveCount(1);
    
    // Search
    await page.fill('[data-testid="template-search"]', 'refactor');
    await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="template-card"]').first())
      .toContainText('Refactoring Expert');
  });
  
  test('should apply template to create agent', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/agents/new');
    await page.click('[data-testid="template-card-refactoring"]');
    await page.click('[data-testid="use-template"]');
    await page.fill('[data-testid="agent-name"]', 'My Refactor Agent');
    await page.click('[data-testid="create-agent"]');
    
    await expect(page).toHaveURL(/\/agents\/[a-z0-9]+/);
    await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="agent-model"]'))
      .toContainText('Claude Opus');
  });
  
  test('should create custom template from agent', async ({ page }) => {
    await page.goto('/agents/existing-agent-id');
    await page.click('[data-testid="save-as-template"]');
    await page.fill('[data-testid="template-name"]', 'My Custom Template');
    await page.selectOption('[data-testid="template-category"]', 'custom');
    await page.click('[data-testid="save-template"]');
    
    await expect(page.locator('[data-testid="success-toast"]'))
      .toContainText('Template saved');
  });
});
```
